title: ElementSelectorRemoteConfig
description: Remote-config payload for the @amplitude/element-selector engine. Every field is optional; absent fields fall back to the package's built-in defaults. Customers see the defaults in the remote-config UI and may add or remove patterns as needed. This schema is the canonical contract between the backend remote-config service, the SDK at runtime, and the dashboard's remote-config editor — keep it in sync with the ElementSelectorRemoteConfig TypeScript type in src/types.ts.

enabled.description: Master kill switch. When false (the default), autocapture continues to use the legacy cssPath algorithm. Flip to true to opt the org into the v1 element-selector engine.

explicitTrackingAttribute.description: HTML attribute customers can set on an element to anchor the selector on that element explicitly. Empty value on the attribute is treated as a suppression signal ('ignore this element's id even if it looks stable'). Defaults to 'data-amp-track-id'. Empty string here is rejected and falls back to the default.

items.description: JavaScript regex source string. Invalid patterns are dropped (with a warn log when a logger is wired into the SDK) — they don't crash the engine.

autogeneratedIdPatterns.description: Full replacement (not merge) for the built-in autogenerated-id pattern list. An empty array is an explicit 'disable autogen filtering' signal — not 'use the defaults'. The default list catches React useId (':r5:'), Radix ('radix-*'), Headless UI ('headlessui-*'), MUI internal ids ('mui-*'), hex-only ids ≥16 chars, trailing long digit runs, and any string with 4+ consecutive digits.

items.description: JavaScript regex source string. Invalid patterns are dropped (with a warn log when a logger is wired into the SDK) — they don't crash the engine.

unstableClassPatterns.description: Full replacement (not merge) for the built-in unstable-class pattern list. An empty array is an explicit 'disable filtering' signal. The default list covers Tailwind utilities (p-4, w-full, bg-blue-500, hover:*, md:*, etc.), CSS-in-JS / build-hash classes (css-*, sc-*, jsx-*, CSS modules), and library runtime state classes (swiper-slide-active, is-active, Mui-selected, data-state-*).

maxAncestorWalkDepth.description: Defensive throttle on the ancestor walk. When omitted (or 0 / negative / NaN / Infinity at runtime), the walk runs all the way to <html>. Positive integers cap the number of ancestors the orchestrator considers — useful for catastrophically deep DOMs where uniqueness checks could become expensive.

shadowDomEnabled.description: Master kill switch for shadow-DOM piercing. When false (the default), generated selectors never cross a shadow boundary and are byte-identical to the non-shadow output. When true, a target inside open shadow root(s) gets a composite selector whose per-tree segments are joined by ' >>> ' and re-resolved with resolveSelector. Independent of `enabled` — shadow support can be rolled out separately from the v1 engine.

maxShadowDomDepth.description: Maximum number of shadow-boundary crossings the engine traverses when shadowDomEnabled is true. Defaults to 1 (a single shadow level). Clamped at runtime into [1, 10]: values below 1 clamp up to 1, values above 10 clamp down to 10. A target nested deeper than the budget yields a best-effort selector for the outermost in-budget shadow host.
